Note: 1200: valparaiso lt brg SExS 8mi Noon: Valparaiso, Chili.
0415: Made Juan Fernandez on port bow.
1745: Pt Quedal bore ExN 1mi. It makes more sense that this was observed at 1945.
1000: Anchored. Off San Carlos.  Aguy Pt NxW1/8W, Cochinos Is? ExN3/4N

Other: 1600: 2nd Cutter ran a race with a whale boat from the shore, from the Mole and around the buoy and back, and beat her by two minutes.
2200: A large fire broke out on shore.

Note: 1645: Got Underway. With USS Onward in tow.
2100: Took Onward in tow 2120: Took departure S Antonio Lt brg SSW 1mi
2200: Cast off Onward and bore up to SSW course.

Other: 0000: Water extremely phosphorescent. Observed before daylight a brighter phosphorescent line about two inches wide extending about fifteen fathoms on each bow. No ripples on the water. 2000 Water extreamely phosphorescent

Note: 1700: Took departure  Quiriquina Lighthouse brg SxW 6mi 1730 WNW

1300: From this page on where there are two entries in the Baro column I have only transcribed the FIRST of the two readings. Later in the logs the entries change from one entry to two entries at will. Some of the single entries seem to favour the previous second entry but some follow on from the previous first entry. Go figure. %^(  Stuart

Other: 1500: From this point on SOME Baro readings are double. NO explanation given in the log.
0230: Saw a very bright meteor of several seconds duration. Numerous meteors seen during watch.
2340: Observed a brilliant meteor to the Northward.

Note: 0000: Mid to Merid block has 13 entries. To fit in I have not entered the first line.
1645: Made Magdalina Is one point on port bow, cc to W1/2N
0205: Sighted an island on port qtr brg ENE aby 7mi
2100: baro has a comment at 2100 that maybe indication its the aneroid barometer. Reading seem to have gone back to single entries from this point on.
0040: Made out Tahiti Is brg SW

People: 1100: HRH Prince Alfred, Duke of Edingurgh visited the ship as Captain of the Galatea.

Note: 1420: South end of Euaigi Is brg SE1/4E, Northern end ESE7/8S cc NxE
0900: Sighted Matuku Is stbd bow brg NWxW. 0945 sighted Tototya Is + Moala Is brg N & N+W
0000: Dropped the 16th from the time on account of crossing the Meridian 180 into East latitude
0530: Kadavu Is came into sight, cc NNW
1335: Anchored. Lavuka Hbr, Lavuka Is. 1330: Anchored. Lavuka Hbr, Lavuka Is.

Flora&Fauna: Noon: A school of fin back whales swimming around the ship during watch.

Note: 0000: During squalls and heavy seas, sustained damage to fore top mast and other damage elsewhere. Laing to most of the day and drifting E+S and E about 1knot/hr
0000: Strong winds frmo Sd+Wd, frequent squalls of considerable force, heavy seas. Lying to & drifting to Sd+Ed at rate of 1kn/hr
1745: Sighted Lord Howe Island. Balls Pyramid brg WxN
0400: Made out Outer South Hd lt brg Sw

People: 1500: Colonial Governor visited the ship, manned yards & dressed ship with the English Ensign at the main during hi sstay on board.

Note: 1800: Departed South Head Lt brg WSW 5mi.
Cape Palliser brg NxW1/4W Mag 31/2 miles at 1735.
0000: Added this day having crossed the 180th meridian into West Longitude
0800: Heading NE+E to NE+N & drifting NNW 1 knot
1900: Assumption made that the Baro readings should be 30xx not 29xx till 2400hrs.

Other: 0800: At 8 o'clock today being the anniversary of a Peruvian victory, dressed the ship with flags at the mast head in accordance with their request.

Ships: 0230: Master of the American ship !Kendrick Fish! came on board and reported his vessel was on fire; sent two boats to his assistance. Sent a 315lb kedge anchor to her during mid-4 watch, watch engaged in towing her into shore water and attempting to extinguish the fire. 1200 working party returned. At 1300 the Kendrick Fish sank in 4 fms water having been scuttled to extinguish the fire.

Note: 1545: took departure San Lorenzo brg ExS 8mi
1725: Made Hormigas de Afuera islands. 1735 cc W1/2N
0530: Made Pelado Is brg NExE cc NExN
0800: Cross brgs. Pelado brg SxE3/8E and Mazorque, E1/4N

Ships: 2130: Sighted USS Knack. Both vessels stopped and Captain Eastman came on board at 2200, left at 2300.

Note: 1545: Anchored. Huacho Bay. Peru. Casquin Pt Brg NW1/2W, Huacho Hd SSW
0630: Took departure Pelado Is brg S 1/2mi cc SExE
